commands.author: TheAngryPit
commands.review_summary:
This PR requires `ToolsEffectiveParams` with a nonblank `sessionKey` for `oc.tools.effective()`, exports that params type, and adds SDK regression coverage for missing params before RPC.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +17. Total +36 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main accepts `oc.tools.effective()` without params and forwards it to RPC, while the Gateway schema, handler, docs, and tests require `sessionKey`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can correct the misplaced public JSDoc without changing runtime behavior; maintainers still own compatibility acceptance and merge timing.

target.title: Track Windows 25H2 build 26200.8728 MXC validation
target.body:
## Summary

This tracks a staged Windows validation effort from a Portuguese Windows Release Preview 25H2 machine on build `26200.8728`.

The work is intentionally split into small reviewable PRs:

- PR1: locale-safe Windows known-folder tests and invariant technical token formatting.
- PR2: MXC SDK `0.7.0` / Windows build `26200.8728` processcontainer validation, direct MXC runtime proof, and isolated Companion MCP-only proof.
- PR3, planned follow-up: full Gateway-mode and installed Companion validation after we run that broader test path safely.

This issue should stay open after PR1 and PR2 if PR3 has not landed yet.

## Problem

There are two related but separable problems:

- Localized Windows installations can expose known folders through localized or redirected paths. Tests that assert English path suffixes can fail even when behavior is correct.
- OpenClaw currently gates MXC availability too narrowly for this Windows 25H2 Release Preview build. The Microsoft MXC SDK probe shows usable processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL support locally on `26200.8728`, but OpenClaw rejects the build before runtime validation can proceed.

## PR1 scope

PR1 is a small test/formatting reliability change:

- Compare generated sandbox grants against OS-provided known-folder paths instead of English suffixes.
- Use invariant culture for compact technical token count formatting.
- Serialize tests that mutate shared `AppVersionInfo.TestOverride` state.

PR1 intentionally does not change MXC availability, Gateway behavior, WSL setup, installed Companion state, or command execution policy.

## PR2 scope

PR2 validates the current MXC path narrowly and explicitly:

- Update `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` to `0.7.0`.
- Replace the single hardcoded build gate with an explicit processcontainer support table.
- Add processcontainer support for `26200.8728+`.
- Preserve processcontainer support for `26300.8289+`.
- Keep `isolation_session` separately gated to `26300.8553+`.
- Emit SDK `0.7.0` config schema `0.7.0-alpha`.
- Keep AppContainer-DACL fallback compatible by granting only backend-safe PATH directories and omitting nonexistent denied-path roots.
- Reject custom `system.run` env in MXC mode until this Windows MXC backend accepts non-empty `process.env`.
- Fail closed when sandboxing is enabled but MXC is unavailable or disappears at runtime.
- Keep sandboxed MXC omitted-shell execution on Windows PowerShell 5.1 for the current AppContainer-DACL tier, while host opt-out can still use PowerShell 7 when explicitly outside the sandbox.

## Local runtime proof

Local proof for PR2 has been run on Windows build `26200.8728`:

- Direct MXC harmless command smoke passed.
- Constrained filesystem/access smoke passed.
- Isolated Companion MCP-only smoke passed from local build output with isolated data directories.
- Temporary MCP `system.run` smoke returned `OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_RUN_MXC_OK`.
- The smoke helper validates MXC containment evidence including `executor=mxc-direct-appc`, `contained=True`, config `version=0.7.0-alpha`, `envKeys=[]`, `containment=mxc`, and absence of `UNCONTAINED`.
- No permanent `system.run` enablement.
- No installed Companion replacement was performed.

## Validation so far

- Build passed locally for the changed surfaces.
- Focused changed-surface tests passed.
- Direct MXC runtime smoke passed.
- Isolated Companion MCP-only runtime smoke passed.
- autoreview found issues in the draft patch; the fixes were applied.
- Final autoreview completed with no accepted/actionable findings.

## Not claimed by PR1/PR2

These are intentionally left for PR3 or later validation:

- Full Gateway-mode Companion pairing/runtime proof.
- Installed Companion replacement/profile migration proof.
- End-to-end product proof.
- Whole-suite green status on this local Windows environment.

## Planned PR3

PR3 should run after PR1 and PR2 are reviewed, unless maintainers prefer a different order.

Planned PR3 goal:

- Launch/test the real Gateway-mode path safely.
- Validate normal node/Companion behavior beyond MCP-only preflight.
- Decide whether installed Companion replacement/profile migration needs code changes or only documented validation.
- Add any resulting fixes as a separate PR instead of expanding PR2.

## Scope guard

PR1 and PR2 should remain reviewable and bounded. If broader Gateway-mode or installed-Companion issues appear, they should be tracked under this issue and handled by PR3 rather than expanding PR2.
target.author: TheAngryPit

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
